Curriculum Vitae

Download PDF

Education

  1. 2025 – Present Cambridge, MA
    GPA: 5.0/5.0

    B.S. in Physics / Artificial Intelligence & Decision Making

    Massachusetts Institute of Technology

    • FortyFive AI Lab (Dr. Ge Yang) — RL, humanoid whole-body control, embodied AI
    • Prof. Tommi Jaakkola's group — diffusion models and molecular dynamics
  2. 2024 – 2025 Beijing, China
    GPA: 3.95/4.00

    Undergraduate in Computer Science

    Tsinghua University — IIIS (Yao Class)

    Advisor: Prof. Huazhe Xu

    • Transferred to MIT after freshman year
    • Research on learning-based robot manipulation, sim-to-real transfer

Experience

  1. Feb 2026 – Present Cambridge, MA

    Undergraduate Researcher — Diffusion Models & Molecular Dynamics

    MIT, supervised by Prof. Tommi Jaakkola

    • Researching the intersection of diffusion models and molecular dynamics.
  2. Oct 2025 – Present Cambridge, MA

    Undergraduate Researcher — Reinforcement Learning & Embodied AI

    FortyFive AI Lab, MIT, supervised by Dr. Ge Yang

    • Developing RL and imitation learning pipelines for humanoid whole-body control; investigating scaling laws for policy performance and generalization.
    • Contributed to productionization of Vuer (3D visualization) and ML-Dash (experiment tracking & data storage).
  3. Mar 2025 – Aug 2025 Beijing, China

    Undergraduate Researcher — Learning-based Control

    Tsinghua University, supervised by Prof. Huazhe Xu

    • Investigated RIR (RL to Imitation to Real-world) framework for robot manipulation on Franka arm, leveraging PPO and DrQ-v2.
    • Designed multi-stage approach for specialist training and multitask generalization via imitation learning and sim-to-real transfer.

Gold Medal — 1st Place in Theory

54th International Physics Olympiad (IPhO)
